reasoning: The question strictly requires a *formal* declaration of war between India and Pakistan by the end of 2025. Formal declarations of war have become practically obsolete in international relations since WWII, as nations prefer to engage in conflicts under the guise of self-defense (Article 51 of the UN Charter) to avoid broader diplomatic and legal consequences. Additionally, both India and Pakistan are nuclear-armed, which provides a massive deterrent against full-scale, formally declared wars; their last major conflict (Kargil, 1999) involved no formal declaration. Given the knowledge cutoff is October 26, 2025, there are effectively only ~2 months left in the year. A formal declaration of two nuclear-armed states within a 2-month window, barring any pre-existing apocalyptic escalation, is astronomically unlikely. Thus, the probability is essentially 0; I am assigning a floor value of 0.02 based on system constraints.
